summary: A single-story rectangular bunker or storage structure built into the ground with a flat roof. The building features a concrete foundation and perimeter, stone brick walls with decorative wood banding, and a row of small barred windows along the front facade. The structure is partially sunken with visible earth embankment on sides.
rebuild_hints: Build rectangular frame 16×8 blocks at ground level
Excavate or build down 2-3 blocks to create sunken appearance
Layer walls: stonebrick primary, with wood plank horizontal stripes every 2-3 rows
Install 4-5 barred windows evenly spaced on front face (use iron bars + glass)
Create concrete/light gray perimeter foundation around base
Top with flat roof using stone slabs and wood trim
Add earth/dirt embankment around exterior sides
Windows appear to be 2×2 blocks with iron bar overlay
uncertainties: Precise window spacing and count varies by angle
Roof interior composition unclear (may be hollow or filled)
Depth of excavation/sunken portion estimated from perspective
Whether structure extends below visible ground level
Exact concrete vs. stone material distinction on foundation
